0.q: The fprB mutant has defects in [4Fe-4S] cluster biogenesis, as shown by reduced activities of [4Fe-4S] cluster-containing enzymes.

1.q: PB-induced expression of fprB was abolished in the ΔiscR mutant and was restored in the ΔiscR mutant complemented with a pIscR plasmid. The results indicate that IscR is the transcription regulator responsible for oxidant-inducible expression of fprB.
